successCriteria.description: Create `src/shared/types/leave.types.ts` with all 7 TypeScript enums: `LeaveType` (ANNUAL, SICK, MATERNITY, PATERNITY, UNPAID, OTHER), `LeaveStatus` (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED), `NotificationType` (LEAVE_REQUESTED, LEAVE_APPROVED, LEAVE_REJECTED, LEAVE_CANCELLED), `AuditAction` (CREATED, UPDATED, DELETED, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED), `EntityType` (LEAVE_REQUEST, LEAVE_BALANCE, LEAVE_POLICY, EMPLOYEE), `EmployeeRole` (EMPLOYEE, MANAGER, HR_ADMIN), `EmploymentStatus` (ACTIVE, INACTIVE, TERMINATED, ON_LEAVE).

Create `src/shared/error.types.ts` with 4 error classes: `NotFoundError` (extends Error, constructor takes entityName: string and id: string), `ValidationError` (extends Error, constructor takes message: string and optional fieldErrors: Array<{ field: string; message: string }>), `ForbiddenError` (extends Error, constructor takes message: string), `ConflictError` (extends Error, constructor takes message: string).

Create `src/shared/base.repository.ts` with an abstract class `BaseRepository<T>` that accepts a Knex instance via constructor and declares abstract method signatures: `findById(id: string): Promise<T | null>`, `findAll(filter?: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<T[]>`, `create(data: Partial<T>): Promise<T>`, `update(id: string, data: Partial<T>): Promise<T>`, `delete(id: string): Promise<void>`.

Create `tests/unit/shared/error.types.test.ts` with Vitest unit tests covering: NotFoundError (correct message formatting with entityName and id), ValidationError (message and fieldErrors array), ForbiddenError (message), ConflictError (message), and instanceof checks for each error class.

constraints: All database access must go through repository interfaces (GP-001). The BaseRepository<T> abstract class must use Knex for database operations.
TypeScript strict mode: no implicit any, strict null checks. Use `unknown` with type guards instead of `any` (HARNESS constraint no-any).
Test framework is Vitest (per AGENTS.md and the intent). HARNESS.json declares Jest but AGENTS.md and the intent text both specify Vitest — Vitest takes precedence as the more specific directive.
No hardcoded secrets, passwords, API keys, or tokens (HARNESS constraint no-hardcoded-secrets).
Do not add @gestalt/* packages as project dependencies (HARNESS constraint no-gestalt-internal-deps).
outOfScope: Phase 3 — LeavePolicy model, repository, service, and index (src/modules/policy/)
Phase 6 — AuditLog and Notification models and repositories
Phase 10 — Leave routes with RBAC middleware
Any database migrations, table creation, or schema definitions — this phase is purely TypeScript types and abstract classes
Any runtime business logic, service orchestration, or API endpoints

ambiguities.description: Knex dependency: The intent says to use "the Knex type from the existing project setup" but the project currently uses `pg` Pool directly (src/shared/db/connection.ts). Knex is not installed as a dependency. The code-agent must either install Knex or adapt BaseRepository to use the existing `pg` Pool.
options: Install Knex as a dependency and use Knex types in BaseRepository
Adapt BaseRepository to use the existing pg Pool instead of Knex
Use a generic database client interface that abstracts over both

ambiguities.description: LeaveStatus.PENDING vs SUBMITTED: The intent defines `LeaveStatus.PENDING` but the ARCHITECTURE.md lifecycle diagram and business rules use `SUBMITTED` for the same state (the state after an employee submits a leave request). This naming inconsistency between the phase-1 intent and the broader architecture could cause confusion in later phases.
options: Use PENDING as specified in the intent (authoritative for Phase 1)
Use SUBMITTED to align with ARCHITECTURE.md
Add both PENDING and SUBMITTED as aliases

ambiguities.description: EmploymentStatus.ON_LEAVE: The intent defines `EmploymentStatus.ON_LEAVE` but the ARCHITECTURE.md Employee lifecycle only shows ACTIVE, INACTIVE, TERMINATED. The ON_LEAVE status is not reflected in the architecture's state transition diagram.
options: Include ON_LEAVE as specified in the intent
Omit ON_LEAVE to align with ARCHITECTURE.md lifecycle
Keep ON_LEAVE and note it as an extension to the architecture

ambiguities.description: File naming convention: The intent specifies `src/shared/error.types.ts` and `src/shared/base.repository.ts` (dot-separated), while ARCHITECTURE.md references `src/shared/errors.ts` and `src/shared/baseRepository.ts` (camelCase). The intent is authoritative for this phase but the inconsistency may cause confusion.
options: Use dot-separated names as specified in the intent (error.types.ts, base.repository.ts)
Use camelCase names from ARCHITECTURE.md (errors.ts, baseRepository.ts)
